citations: Scryfall: WAR-55 oracle text references 'a Jace planeswalker'; ZNR-63 type_line carries the Jace planeswalker subtype
MTG Wiki: Jace Beleren character page (jace beleren, mind sculptor, the living guildpact)
Both cards already carry ip_verified: true with suspected_ip: Jace Beleren
Existing edge in cards/.../55-jace-s-triumph.md ## Connections section explicitly links the two depictions
candidates.case: Two trivia-passed cards already canonically depict the same character across two sets (WAR + ZNR) at two distinct narrative beats (trapped under the Immortal Sun vs. pursuing Nahiri at the Murasa Skyclave) by two different artists. The card-to-card edge has already been drawn, which means a future Edgelord pass on a third Jace card would write a duplicate edge pointing at an absent center. This is the textbook Mr. Nodeley trigger — promote to character node, attach both cards via the appears_on pattern (matches nicol-bolas precedent exactly).
candidates.caveats: An enlightened-Edgelord pass would have to refactor the existing card-to-card connection bullet to instead route through the character node, which is non-trivial cleanup. Also, the character is large enough across Magic's history that someone might argue for splitting by era (pre-Mending Jace vs. post-Mending vs. Living Guildpact); recommend single node with aspect-clusters in the body like the Bolas-Falls cluster section in nicol-bolas.md.

citations: MTG Wiki — Duskmourn: House of Horror set page; the plane is a single house-sized world ruled by Valgavoth
Wizards: Top of the Duskmourning Part 1 + Part 2 (Mark Rosewater, 2024) — designer-confirmed framing of the plane as a 1970s/80s/90s slasher-and-haunted-house homage
Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Duskmourn — primary lore source for Valgavoth, the consumed-suns motif, and the survivor-archetype design conceit
Six trivia-passed cards in the BBL corpus all explicitly anchor flavor or narrative on the haunted-house plane and Valgavoth's wing-shadow appetite
candidates.case: Six trivia-passed cards from a single set all share a load-bearing plane-as-plot concept that survives every tag-collapse pass: every card's trivia reads 'inside the House, ruled by Valgavoth.' That is genuine narrative cohesion, not a tag bridge. Without this hub, a future bundle assembled around modern-horror or haunted-house thesis would have to write the same plane-context paragraph six times. Promoting to hub lets bundle prose load by reference. The Survivor flavor cycle (Frantic Strength's Taros, Horrid Vigor's Sunrise) is the human-cost layer of the same anchor.
candidates.caveats: Three competing framings could split this node: (a) duskmourn-house as the plane / setting (recommended); (b) valgavoth as the canonical antagonist character (would inherit fewer cards because not every card depicts him); (c) survivors as a faction-character (the named flavor speakers Taros, Sunrise, Zimone Wola). Edgelord should write the plane-node first; the antagonist and the survivor-faction could become sibling nodes once the corpus accumulates more depictions of each.

citations: MTG Wiki — Orzhov Syndicate guild page; the white-black 'Church of Deals' / debt-as-power guild
Existing cards/_symbols/orzhov-signet.md cites the canonical eclipsed-sun signet and faction lore
Hipsters of the Coast: 'Behind the Front Lines' preview (2019) — Charity Extractor depicted as generic Orzhov knight per Wizards art-brief
Hipsters of the Coast: Torbeck (2020) — Vito as Legion of Dusk / Orzhov-coded heretic vampire hierophant
All six cards' trivia sections name Orzhov by faction explicitly
candidates.case: Six trivia-passed cards already anchored to the same canonical guild-as-institution. The orzhov-signet symbol exists, but the symbol is the iconography; the SYNDICATE (the institution-as-political-actor) is the larger concept that Vito (Ixalan Legion of Dusk, Orzhov-adjacent), Mortify (Hilgur the Orzhov euthanist), Charity Extractor (Orzhov tax-collector), Pitiless Pontiff, Tithe Drinker, and Lawmage's Binding (Azorius lawmages, but the institutional-religion-as-extraction theme aligns) all share. The Tithe bundle was already authored around this institution. The hub anchors a load-bearing thesis for the BBL brand.
candidates.caveats: Risks subsuming the orzhov-signet symbol — an enlightened-Edgelord would have to clarify the symbol-vs-hub relationship in both MDs (signet = iconographic primitive, syndicate = the institution that brands its hierarchy with the signet). Also, Lawmage's Binding depicts Azorius enforcers, not Orzhov; a careful read might split off Azorius Senate as a sibling hub later. Weaker candidate for a strict 6-card members list — Lawmage and Vito could be argued as sibling-but-not-member.

citations: Wizards: 'Waging War of the Spark, Part 1' by Mark Rosewater (2019-04-01) — designer-confirmed cycle template: one static/triggered ability plus one negative-loyalty ability, 20 uncommons across the set
MTG Wiki — War of the Spark set page; lists all 20 uncommon planeswalkers as a designed cycle
Both cards' Connections sections already cite the Rosewater article as the canonical designer-confirmed source
Scryfall: rarity=uncommon, type_line includes Planeswalker, set=WAR — returns the 20-card cycle
candidates.case: Designer-confirmed cycle membership is a first-class graph concept (cf. the bbl-edgelord 'designer-confirmed flavor cycle' edge category). 2 trivia-passed members and 18 more cards waiting to be added to the corpus eventually. Without a cycle node, every new Edgelord pass between two uncommon WAR walkers writes the same 'we are both members of Rosewater's confirmed 20-walker cycle' edge — pure duplication. A 'cycle' node would let cards declare cycle membership in frontmatter and unlock bundle-cohesion calls like 'WAR uncommon planeswalker draft cube of the underdogs.'
candidates.caveats: This requires inventing a new graph dimension — neither hub nor symbol nor character nor artist quite fits a 'cycle' concept. A more conservative move is to call this a hub (cycle as a curatorial concept) and accept that its boundaries are designer-set rather than thematic. Type = other because it is genuinely a new layer the bbl-edgelord agent spec anticipates ('rarely - a brand-new node-type Alex hasn't built yet'). Caller should decide whether to extend the graph to support cycles, or fold this into hubs with a brand_weight: cycle annotation.

citations: Scryfall: WAR-220 (List reprint) type_line Legendary Planeswalker — Tamiyo; ip_verified: true
MTG Wiki — Tamiyo character page (Avacyn Restored, Eldritch Moon, WAR, Strixhaven, Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty, Modern Horizons 3 arc)
Scryfall: MH3-72 is a Saga that names Tamiyo in the title and depicts her story-circle scrolls
Tamiyo, Collector of Tales trivia already documents the character's full canonical arc from the Avacyn Restored debut to her Compleated form's death
candidates.case: Two enriched corpus cards (one trivia-passed, one vision-only) both anchor on the same canonical moonfolk-scholar planeswalker who appears across 8+ Wizards sets. The MH3 Saga is one of the most recent printings of the character; the List reprint is the WAR-era Phyrexian-precedent printing. Promoting to character node lets a future MH3-72 trivia pass write into the character entry rather than duplicating biographical context. The character arc (Moon Sage → Field Researcher → Collector → Compleated → killed by the Wanderer) is large enough to warrant the aspect-cluster pattern from nicol-bolas.md.
candidates.caveats: MH3-72 is enriched but not yet trivia-passed (ip_verified: false in its frontmatter; the warning callout still asks a reviewer to confirm); the case rests partially on a card whose IP is high-confidence-unverified rather than canonically locked. Mr. Nodeley could defensibly say 'wait for trivia pass on 72-tamiyo-meets-the-story-circle before promoting' — but Tamiyo's identity is unambiguous from the card title alone, and the symmetric character-node precedent (nicol-bolas was promoted on three trivia-passed cards) suggests one trivia-passed plus one enriched-with-named-IP is enough.

citations: MTG Wiki — Duskmourn: House of Horror; Valgavoth named as the demon-ruler whose wings condemn everyone inside to feed his appetite for terror
Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Duskmourn — Valgavoth as the canonical antagonist of the plane's storyline
Multiple corpus cards mention Valgavoth by name in their trivia (Slavering Branchsnapper's consumed-suns lore, Manifest Dread's set lore, Horrid Vigor's slasher-villain framing)
Duskmourn's Domination flavor text directly names Valgavoth: 'All who enter the House hear Valgavoth's whispers'
candidates.case: Seven Duskmourn cards in the corpus reference Valgavoth as the architect of the plane or as the off-frame antagonist. A character node anchors all of them through a single canonical figure rather than scattering the citation chain. The Bolas-Falls precedent (off-frame antagonist as anchor for cards that don't strictly depict him) applies cleanly here.
candidates.caveats: This is a sibling rather than a substitute for the proposed duskmourn-house hub (rank 2). Valgavoth is the antagonist; the plane is the setting. Both could exist; if only one is promoted in this pass the plane-hub is broader and load-bearing for more bundles. If the caller treats this as a competing candidate to rank 2, prefer rank 2 — but the structurally-correct move is to promote both and let Edgelord draw a related_characters edge from the hub to the character. Listed separately here because the prompt asked for 10 candidates, and the prompt's strength-test allows two related nodes to both stand.

citations: Scryfall artist field on each card: 'Slawomir Maniak'
Despark is a designated WAR story-spotlight card painted by Maniak (per trivia: 'previewed by The Vorthos Cast', the magic-story-and-art podcast routing per Wizards' convention)
Tithe Drinker is the canonical second cell of the orzhov-signet symbol (per cards/_symbols/orzhov-signet.md)
Angrath, Captain of Chaos is a WAR uncommon-planeswalker-cycle member, also by Maniak
candidates.case: Four corpus cards by the same artist, three trivia-passed, two of which (Despark, Angrath) are part of the same WAR set's design-confirmed cycles. Maniak is also the artist on Tithe Drinker, the second canonical orzhov-signet appearance. The artist's stylistic coherence across these four pieces (chiaroscuro action, action-with-fallen-grandeur registers) is itself a load-bearing graph signal for any bundle considering art-direction-coherent cohesion. Mirrors the jenn-ravenna-tran precedent for promoting an artist when corpus credits accumulate.
candidates.caveats: The bbl-edgelord agent spec explicitly cautions that 'shared-artist-with-thematic-intent' is the weakest valid edge category — used 'only when the artist's stylistic continuity is the point.' For Maniak, the case rests on him painting designer-confirmed cycle members in the SAME set (WAR-190 and WAR-227 both Maniak), which IS Wizards-confirmed art-direction continuity, not Edgelord overreach. Still, this is a weaker rank than the character/hub candidates above and Mr. Nodeley would defer in his sniveliest voice.

citations: Scryfall artist field on each card: 'Kieran Yanner'
MTG Wiki — War of the Spark set page: Triumph cycle painters (all 5 Triumphs by Yanner) and the cycle's role as direct callback to the HOU Defeat cycle (also Yanner)
Jace's Triumph and Nissa's Triumph trivia sections both name Yanner as the designed cycle painter unifying the two-set visual call-and-response
candidates.case: Seven corpus cards by the same artist, two trivia-passed, and the two trivia-passed are members of the Wizards-designer-confirmed Triumph cycle that the artist was specifically art-directed to unify (Yanner painted all five WAR Triumphs and the matching five HOU Defeats they callback to). The stylistic-continuity-is-the-point criterion is met by Wizards' own art direction decision. Promoting Yanner lets any future Triumph or Defeat card edge to the artist node and inherit the cycle context.
candidates.caveats: Same caveat as rank 7 — artist nodes are the weakest category per Edgelord's standards. Yanner's case is slightly stronger than Maniak's because the Wizards-confirmed Triumph cycle's visual unity is the explicit design intent, not just a happens-to-be-in-the-corpus signal. Still rank 7 / 8 weighting is close and the caller may flip them.

citations: Scryfall: ELD-309 type_line Legendary Planeswalker — Oko; ip_verified: true on both cards
MTG Wiki — Oko character page; canonical Lorwyn/Shadowmoor fey planeswalker, antler-form via Wildered Quest novella
ELD-312 oracle text explicitly tutors for 'Oko, the Trickster' by name and flavor text is signed '—Oko'
Existing edge in both cards' ## Connections sections already cites the same character with shared product-line context
candidates.case: Two trivia-passed cards both depict the same character, both from the same Throne of Eldraine planeswalker-deck precon (ELD-309 + ELD-312, sequential collector numbers). The card-to-card edge has already been drawn — once again the textbook Mr. Nodeley trigger where a future third Oko card (Oko, Thief of Crowns; Oko, the Ringleader; etc.) arriving would cause edge duplication. Same pattern as rank 1 (Jace Beleren), one of the canonical planeswalkers most likely to appear in future card additions to the corpus.
candidates.caveats: Two cards from the same product (same precon, same product launch) is structurally weaker than two cards from different sets / different storylines (the Jace Beleren case). One could argue this is closer to an edge-replacement (the card-to-card connection already documents the character) than a node-promotion (would future Oko cards really inherit much from this node?). Mr. Nodeley flags this and asks the caller to consider whether a follow-up Edgelord pass should redirect the existing card-to-card edge to route through the new node.

citations: Wizards: 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' (Mark Rosewater, 2019-10-21) — designer-confirmed fairy-tale-object cycle: Excalibur, the Holy Grail, the Round Table, the glass slipper, the pumpkin carriage, the glass casket, Pinocchio, the Gingerbread Man, the Magic Mirror
Wizards: 'Eldraine or Shine' (Mark Rosewater, 2019-09-09) — Wicked Guardian named explicitly as the Cinderella-cycle stepmother card
Enchanted Carriage trivia documents Rosewater's verbatim quote about the pumpkin carriage and explicitly confirms the cycle membership
Throne of Eldraine set page describes the top-down Grimms' Fairy Tales + Arthurian Legend design conceit
candidates.case: Eleven enriched Throne of Eldraine cards (1 trivia-passed so far: Enchanted Carriage; plus Wicked Guardian + Oko's Hospitality though that's character-Oko not fairy-tale) all share the same Wizards-designer-confirmed fairy-tale-object cycle. Rosewater's preview article explicitly names the cycle and lists the artifacts. The cycle is a designer-confirmed flavor-cluster (the strongest valid edge category per Edgelord's spec), but it has no node — so future bundles built on Eldraine's fairy-tale conceit have to write the same designer-citation chain on every card. Weaker rank than 1-9 because only one corpus card is trivia-passed; the case rests largely on vision-pass tags + the Rosewater citation.
candidates.caveats: Weaker candidate, may not survive Judge review. Only one trivia-passed member (Enchanted Carriage); the eleven enriched-only members carry the case via vision tags and the designer-confirmed cycle citation rather than per-card narrative weight. A more conservative move is to wait until 3+ trivia-passed members exist before promoting. Alternative slug names: 'cinderella-cycle' (too narrow — the cycle includes Snow White's casket, Hansel & Gretel's gingerbread house, Pinocchio etc., not just Cinderella), 'fairy-tale-object' (broader but less anchored to the specific Wizards-named cluster), 'eldraine-iconic-objects' (cycle-as-set-feature framing). Caller should pick the slug; the case for the NODE is stronger than the case for any specific slug.
